festivals.name: Songkran
festivals.description: Thai New Year transforms streets into the world's biggest water fight, washing away the old year's misfortunes.

festivals.name: Yi Peng Lantern Festival
festivals.description: Thousands of paper lanterns rise into the Chiang Mai night sky, creating one of the world's most magical spectacles.

destinations.name: Bangkok
destinations.description: Thailand's electric capital mixes ornate temples and canal boats with world-famous street food and nightlife.

destinations.name: Chiang Mai
destinations.description: Northern Thailand's cultural capital has 300 ancient temples, jungle trekking, and the best Thai cooking schools.

destinations.name: Ayutthaya
destinations.description: A UNESCO island city of crumbling stupa-studded ruins — the former Siamese capital at its peak had 1 million inhabitants.

destinations.name: Chiang Rai
destinations.description: The White Temple (Wat Rong Khun) is Thailand's most otherworldly building, near the infamous Golden Triangle.

destinations.name: Sukhothai
destinations.description: Thailand's first capital has an atmospheric historical park of serene Buddha images and lotus-filled moats.

destinations.name: Pai
destinations.description: A laid-back mountain valley in Mae Hong Son province with hot springs, waterfalls, and a free-spirited village vibe.

destinations.name: Doi Inthanon National Park
destinations.description: Thailand's highest peak at 2,565m holds twin royal chedis, forested trails, and some of Southeast Asia's best birdwatching.

destinations.name: Kanchanaburi
destinations.description: The Bridge on the River Kwai and WWII history meet stunning waterfalls and the lush western wilderness.
